docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Using Emojis
docs.text: I like to drink :beers: after I played :soccer:

docs.title: Admonitions
docs.text:
Example of an admonition/callout with a title:

!!! note "Title of the callout"

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nulla et euismod
nulla. Curabitur feugiat, tortor non consequat finibus, justo purus auctor
massa, nec semper lorem quam in massa.

Collapsible callout:

??? info "Collapsible callout"

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nulla et euismod
nulla. Curabitur feugiat, tortor non consequat finibus, justo purus auctor
massa, nec semper lorem quam in massa.

docs.title: Code examples
docs.text:
An example of a codeblock for Python:

```py title="add_numbers.py" linenums="1"

docs.title: Example usage
docs.text:
result = add_two_numbers(5, 3) print('The sum is:', result)


Example codeblock for JavaScript with lines highlighted:

```js title="concatenate_strings.js" linenums="1" hl_lines="2-4"
// Function to concatenate two strings
function concatenateStrings(str1, str2) {
  return str1 + str2;
}

// Example usage
const result = concatenateStrings("Hello, ", "World!");
console.log("The concatenated string is:", result);

docs.title: Content Tabs
docs.text: This is some examples of content tabs.

docs.title: Generic Content
docs.text:
=== "Plain text"

This is some plain text

=== "Unordered list"

* First item
* Second item
* Third item

=== "Ordered list"

1. First item
2. Second item
3. Third item

docs.title: Code Blocks in Content Tabs
docs.text:
=== "Python"

```py
def main():
    print("Hello world!")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

=== "JavaScript"

```js
function main() {
    console.log("Hello world!");
}

main();
```

docs.title: Flowcharts
docs.text:
graph LR
  A[Start] --> B{Failure?};
  B -->|Yes| C[Investigate...];
  C --> D[Debug];
  D --> B;
  B ---->|No| E[Success!];

docs.title: Sequence Diagrams
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  Server->>Terminal: Send request
  loop Health
      Terminal->>Terminal: Check for health
  end
  Note right of Terminal: System online
  Terminal-->>Server: Everything is OK
  Terminal->>Database: Request customer data
  Database-->>Terminal: Customer data
